name: Common Crawl
key_insight: Non-profit web crawl that powers billion-dollar language models, built by a tiny team with no marketing budget.
notes: The ultimate Stalwart. Free dataset downloaded by virtually every major AI lab.

name: Bright Data
key_insight: Operates a massive proxy network that lets companies scrape the web at scale without getting blocked.
notes: Formerly Luminati. One of the largest proxy networks in the world.

name: ScrapingBee
key_insight: Simple API for developers who need to extract web data without managing proxies themselves.
notes: Developer-friendly alternative to enterprise scraping tools.

name: Apify
key_insight: An app store for web crawlers where developers build and share ready-to-use scraping tools.
notes: YC-backed. Strong marketplace model for scraping automations.

name: Snowflake Data Marketplace
key_insight: Companies sell proprietary datasets inside Snowflake's warehouse; buyers query without downloading.
notes: Part of Snowflake's broader data cloud strategy.

name: AWS Data Exchange
key_insight: Amazon's data marketplace where datasets appear directly in your AWS account.
notes: Tight integration with AWS services makes it frictionless for existing AWS users.

name: Databricks Marketplace
key_insight: Focuses on machine-learning-ready datasets, often pre-cleaned and documented.
notes: Leverages Databricks' strong position in the ML ecosystem.

name: Gretel
key_insight: Creates synthetic versions of sensitive datasets that keep statistical patterns but remove personal information.
notes: Critical for healthcare and finance where privacy laws lock up real data.

name: Hazy
key_insight: Synthetic data generation focused on enterprise compliance and privacy regulations.
notes: UK-based. Strong focus on GDPR and financial compliance.

name: Scale AI
key_insight: The Foxconn of data: global workforce plus software to label at massive scale for autonomous vehicles and defense.
notes: One of the most valuable companies in the data infrastructure space.

name: Appen
key_insight: Australian crowdsourced data labeling company that predates the AI buzzword era.
notes: Publicly traded. Long history in search and voice assistant training data.

name: TELUS International
key_insight: Canadian outsourcing giant with annotation centers worldwide and strict quality control.
notes: Part of TELUS corporation. Emphasizes AI-assisted labeling with human oversight.

name: Labelbox
key_insight: Enterprise 'data factory' where teams upload raw data, define tasks, and manage quality in-house.
notes: Andreessen Horowitz backed. Strong emphasis on AI-assisted pre-labeling.

name: SuperAnnotate
key_insight: Computer vision focused with built-in tools for image segmentation and video tracking.
notes: Strong in autonomous driving and medical imaging verticals.

name: Encord
key_insight: Combines labeling with active learning: suggests which images need human attention most.
notes: YC-backed. Focuses on reducing annotation costs through smart sampling.

name: Dataloop
key_insight: End-to-end platform covering annotation, automation, and model training in one place.
notes: Israeli company. Strong in MLOps integration.

name: iMerit
key_insight: Specializes in medical imaging and autonomous driving with clinically trained annotators.
notes: Premium pricing for high-stakes verticals where mistakes are costly.

name: Sama
key_insight: Certified B-Corp combining ethical AI sourcing with high-quality annotation and fair wages.
notes: Hires workers in East Africa and India with above-market wages. Strong ethical positioning.

name: CloudFactory
key_insight: Blends software with a managed workforce focused on long-term client relationships.
notes: Nepal and Kenya based workforce. Emphasizes partnership over one-off projects.

name: AWS SageMaker Ground Truth
key_insight: Amazon's built-in labeling service that handles workforce or pre-labeling automatically.
notes: Tight integration with SageMaker. Good for teams already deep in AWS.

name: Azure ML Data Labeling
key_insight: Microsoft's equivalent to Ground Truth, integrated with Azure's ML tools.
notes: Part of Azure Machine Learning suite.

name: Label Studio
key_insight: The most popular open-source labeling tool. Self-hosted, community-driven, and extensible.
notes: The ultimate Stalwart in this layer. A single engineer can set it up in an afternoon.

name: CVAT
key_insight: Intel-built tool for video and image annotation at scale. Used by researchers and startups worldwide.
notes: Originally from Intel Open Source. Strong in computer vision research.

name: Argilla
key_insight: Focused on text and NLP data with collaborative labeling and feedback loops for language models.
notes: Spanish team. Growing fast in the LLM fine-tuning community.

name: Apache Airflow
key_insight: The grandfather of data pipelines. Created by Airbnb, now an Apache standard.
notes: Industry standard but increasingly seen as complex. Prefect and Dagster are modern alternatives.

name: Prefect
key_insight: Modern alternative to Airflow built by former Airflow developers who wanted something simpler.
notes: Strong Python-native approach. Good for data science teams.

name: Dagster
key_insight: Treats data pipelines like software with typed inputs and outputs for easier testing and debugging.
notes: Elementl is the company behind it. Emphasizes data quality and observability.

name: DVC
key_insight: Git for data. Versions datasets alongside your code so you can trace exactly which data trained which model.
notes: The ultimate Stalwart in pipeline tooling. Small open-source team, huge impact.

name: MLflow
key_insight: Open standard for tracking experiments, model versions, and parameters. Originally from Databricks.
notes: Widely adopted but Databricks commercial offering adds features.

name: Weights & Biases
key_insight: Commercial platform that logs experiments, visualizes training runs, and compares model versions.
notes: Extremely popular with researchers and deep learning teams. Freemium model.

name: Lambda Labs
key_insight: GPU cloud provider that undercuts AWS on ML workloads. Started as a workstation builder for students.
notes: The Stalwart of GPU access. No enterprise contracts, just affordable H100s by the hour.

name: CoreWeave
key_insight: Former crypto miners who pivoted to AI cloud. Own one of the largest private GPU fleets in the world.
notes: Incredible pivot story. Now a major competitor to cloud hyperscalers for AI training.

name: Together AI
key_insight: Distributed training across many smaller GPUs, making large-model training more accessible.
notes: Strong focus on open-source model training and research.

name: NVIDIA DGX
key_insight: A supercomputer in a box for companies that cannot send data to the cloud.
notes: Popular in defense, finance, and healthcare for air-gapped environments.
